content:
Valentine’s Day fashion in 2026 is bigger than ever. It’s no longer just about roses and chocolates. It has become one of the most exciting seasonal style moments of the year.

Whether you’re dressing up for a romantic dinner, heading out with friends, or planning a cute family celebration, this year’s Valentine outfit trends are all about one thing: comfort meets confidence with a touch of romance.

And the best part? Valentine’s looks aren’t just for couples anymore. The latest outfit trends for women, men, and kids are blooming beautifully across every age group.

Let’s explore the most loved Valentine’s Day outfit ideas for 2026.

Valentine Outfit Trends for Men 2026: Smart Casual with a Bold Pop

Men’s fashion trends in 2026 are focusing on clean silhouettes, smart fits, and subtle Valentine tones.

Instead of loud prints, the season is leaning toward solid romantic shades like red, maroon, and warm earthy colours. A crisp shirt paired with dark trousers creates a confident, put-together outfit that works perfectly even after Valentine’s Day.

This is one of the easiest smart casual outfits for men that feels festive without trying too hard.

Style Tip:

Roll up the sleeves for a relaxed vibe or keep it buttoned-up for a sharper evening-ready finish.

Valentine Outfit Trends for Women 2026: Soft Glam and Statement Reds

When it comes to Valentine’s Day outfits for women, 2026 is embracing effortless glamour with bold romantic colours.

One of the biggest trends this season is stylish tops for women in classic reds, paired with sleek everyday basics. A puff-sleeve red top instantly adds festive charm, while black leggings or fitted pants create a perfectly balanced silhouette.

This look is ideal for:

  • Brunch dates
  • Casual outings
  • Evening celebrations
  • Everyday festive styling

It’s not just trendy. It’s wearable well beyond Valentine’s week.

Style Tip:

Pair your red top with minimal jewellery, a chic handbag, and comfortable flats for an effortlessly polished look.

Valentine Outfit Trends for Boys 2026: Cool, Comfortable and Celebration-Ready

Kids’ fashion in 2026 is all about fun styling that doesn’t compromise on comfort.

For boys, Valentine outfits are seeing a rise in easy, casual combinations, such as bright shirts paired with classic denim. A cheerful red shirt with blue jeans is a timeless go-to look that feels festive, playful, and perfect for family outings or school celebrations.

Parents searching for trendy clothes for boys will love how simple yet stylish this outfit feels.

Style Tip:

Finish the look with sneakers, because kids need outfits made for movement.

Valentine Outfit Trends for Girls 2026: Pretty Dresses with Princess Energy

For little girls, Valentine’s Day is the perfect excuse to dress up and feel special.

In 2026, the trend spotlight is on party wear dresses for girls, featuring flowy silhouettes, bow accents, and romantic festive colours. A red dress remains a timeless Valentine favourite that is sweet, comfortable, and picture-perfect for celebrations.

This style also works beautifully for:

  • Birthdays
  • Family functions
  • Festive events
  • Special occasions

Style Tip:

Add cute sandals and a headband for an instantly charming festive look.

One Trend Everyone Loves in 2026: Family Outfit Coordination

One of the most heartwarming fashion trends growing beyond Valentine’s Day is family outfit coordination.

These are not matching outfits, but coordinated colour stories.

Families are choosing shades like:

  • Red
  • Soft pink
  • Neutrals
  • Warm festive tones

It’s a beautiful way to dress up together for celebrations, casual dinners, festive outings, and Valentine moments.

Family fashion trends in 2026 are truly becoming a style statement of their own.

Dress Up, Step Out and Celebrate Valentine in Style

Valentine’s Day fashion doesn’t have to be complicated.

Sometimes, it’s as simple as choosing an outfit that makes you feel good, whether you’re 6 or 36.

content:
Weddings are a rare occasion where families come together to celebrate love, joy, and shared traditions. When the family looks like they belong together, it creates a more memorable atmosphere. One of the best ways to enhance this sense of unity is through coordinated wedding outfits. They create a cohesive look that tells a visual story. Here, we will explore how to create the perfect family outfits for a wedding event so that every member looks stylish.

For men

While selecting men’s wedding outfits, it can be tricky to balance between tradition and modernity. However, this peach-coloured kurta and pyjama combo, paired with a well-fitted Nehru coat, can be a classic choice. The soft peach is subtle enough to mix with a variety of colours, which makes it a versatile pick. It is a look that stands out in photos but does not steal the show.

For women

Check out this vibrant violet lehenga with intricate white embroidery. It can bring a lot of personality, and add richness to the overall family look. The bright purple colour is eye-catching, but it does not overwhelm. It pairs beautifully with the white embroidery, which adds a nice touch of detail. This look has plenty of flair, but it remains elegant for a wedding celebration.

For boys

Boys can opt for a black tuxedo with a waistcoat featuring golden patterns. It is a timeless yet youthful choice. The sharp black tuxedo adds formality and makes them look smart without feeling over-the-top. The golden details on the tuxedo introduce a bit of sparkle, but it is the kind of touch that feels just right. The contrast between the tuxedo and the other family outfits creates a sense of balance. It adds depth to the overall visual harmony of the family’s look.

For girls

When it comes to the younger girls, comfort and style should go hand in hand. An indigo blue long dress with a cute belt is a perfect fit. It is simple, but the rich colour gives it a depth that resonates well with the adults’ outfits. The belt adds a playful and youthful vibe. It ensures the girls look both stylish and comfortable. It does not overshadow the other outfits but adds a refreshing, lighter touch to the family’s wedding outfits. It is also the kind of dress that is easy to move around in.

title: Popular ways to celebrate Durga Puja
excerpt:
It is that time of the year again when people are ready to welcome Durga Maa with bowed heads and start the Puja festivities. While the Durga Puja celebration primarily happens over four days, the preparations begin months in advance. Here is a look at some popular ways people like to celebrate the major event … 
Continue reading “Popular ways to celebrate Durga Puja”
content:
It is that time of the year again when people are ready to welcome Durga Maa with bowed heads and start the Puja festivities. While the Durga Puja celebration primarily happens over four days, the preparations begin months in advance. Here is a look at some popular ways people like to celebrate the major event every year.

Shopping

Long before the puja pandals are completed, people set out on shopping sprees. This is the time for them to buy stylish outfits for themselves, their families and other loved ones. From the latest and trendy dresses to shirts and denim, the Durga Puja shopping is almost an event itself. And, people do not just buy clothes but they also spend considerable time deciding each aspect of their look on each day of the puja. For example, most people opt for traditional and ethnic outfits, accessories and shoes for Mahashtami.

Pandal hopping

Men, women and children alike dress up in their new clothes and venture outside to visit the many pandals strewn across the city. Each pandal is designed differently following trending themes or important events of the recent past. The intricate artistry and craftsmanship is truly breathtaking to behold. Pandal hopping is such a popular activity that the sheer crowd in some of the pandals can seem intimidating. But regardless of the difficulties, setting out to witness Maa Durga in all her glory is a favourite way to spend the puja days for many.

Attending the festivities

Almost every lane has its own Durga Puja celebrations. Residents are invited to participate in all aspects of the Puja, right from the Pushpanjali on Mahashtami mornings to cultural programmes and events. People often rehearse and practise for these events for months. Durga Puja is an incredible time of inclusivity where everyone is welcome to join in on the fun.

Relishing on delicacies

In the middle of all that pandal hopping, people need refreshments too. With food stalls set up all around and many restaurants offering special menus, people are happy to indulge. From Biryani to Indo-Chinese and from kathi rolls to ice creams, the four days of Durga Puja is a time when everyone forgets all about their diet. They indulge in local and foreign delicacies to celebrate the festive season to the fullest.

title: The History and Culture of the Rajjo Festival in Odisha
excerpt:
Odisha is well known for its cultural magnificence and heritage. Here, we pick up just one such festival which is known as Rajjo Festival. It is also known by other names like the Raja Festival and Raja Parba. Here, let us find out more about this festival under the following sub-heads: Dates of Rajjo Festival … 
Continue reading “The History and Culture of the Rajjo Festival in Odisha”
content:
Odisha is well known for its cultural magnificence and heritage. Here, we pick up just one such festival which is known as Rajjo Festival. It is also known by other names like the Raja Festival and Raja Parba.

Here, let us find out more about this festival under the following sub-heads:

  • Dates of Rajjo Festival in 2023
  • Its Origin
  • Significance of the Raja Festival
  • How is it celebrated?

So, let us take one sub-topic at a time, and know about each in brief.

 

Dates of Rajjo Festival in 2023
This is a three-day festival and this year in 2023, the same will be celebrated on the following dates-

  • ✔ Pahili Raja – Date: June 14th 2023
  • ✔ Raja Sankranti – Date: June 15th 2023
  • ✔ Basi Raja – Date: June 16th, 2023
  • ✔ Basumati Snana – Date: June 17th 2023
Its Origin
The Rajjo Festival is being celebrated in Odisha for centuries. It is an ancient festival and its origin is a belief that is indeed powerful. It is believed that just as a woman undergoes a menstrual cycle, the Earth does too. As such, this time, it requires adequate rest, nurturing, and rejuvenation. In other words, it is related to the renewal of life and rebirth of the Earth. So, in Odisha, the kings and queens celebrated the occasion so that they could rejuvenate their land and subjects. As tradition goes, many restrictions are imposed on the woman folk. This is even more popular among the unmarried girls.

The restrictions that the woman folk must follow include right from not doing any work inside the kitchen, cleaning and mopping floors, walking without shoes, and combing hair. However, you will find that in many urban settings, not all of these restrictions are followed and people have become liberal in many impositions.

Significance of the Raja Festival

In our country, menstruation is still taboo and is the least talked about. As such, this festival is a unique occasion and celebrates Bhudevi, wife of Lord Jagannath.

Once the Earth undergoes a menstrual cycle for three days, on the fourth, she gets a ceremonial bath. The first day of the Rajjo Festival is known as Pahili Raja. The second day of the festivity is called Raja Sankranti. The third day is known in the local dialect of Bhuin Dahana. The last day which marks the day of the ceremonial bath is known as Basumata Puja, also popularly called Basumata Gadhua.

How is it celebrated?

Aside from its traditional and cultural significance, the festival is a symbol of solidarity and unity within the community. It is a time to celebrate, share, and enjoy the occasion.

Food
Cuisine or food plays a crucial role in the occasion on all four days. One of the main recipes or dishes that is considered mandatory for the festival is known as poda pitha. It is made up of jaggery, coconut, and rice. It is a sweet cake. Apart from this main dish, the other delicacies that you can indulge in during these days include Chakuli Pitha, Manda Pitha, and Poda Arisa.
Dressing up for elegance

The traditional dress code is followed during the Rajjo festival in Odisha. While men are expected to wear Gamchas and dhotis, which are traditional attire worn on the waist. Women adorn themselves in colorful sarees, vibrant colors, wear jewelry, and put flowers in their hair. Interestingly, the attire worn by women is significant as it symbolizes fertility and womanhood.

Dance and music galore

Any festival is not complete without music and dance. You will get to see traditional forms of music that include Dalkhai, Pala, and Ghoda Nacha. While you will catch a glimpse of women dancing, men play traditional instruments like mridanga and dhol.

Conclusion:
The Raja or Rajjo Festival of Odisha is not just a celebration of the rich cultural heritage of Odisha, it is the time when all family members come together for a reunion.

title: The Significance of Color in Lagan Fashion: Symbolism and Meaning

content:
When it comes to weddings, we have always believed that matches are made in Heaven. Undoubtedly, they are, and Indian weddings are well known for their vibrancy and aesthetic décor that is complemented by colors, that are evident in the décor and trousseau of the bride and groom. Let us delve deeper into a few of the most prominent colors in wedding fashion, their symbolism, and their significance.

 

1.Red
Red is the most used color in a wedding. In most cases, the wedding attire is red, especially for the bride. For the groom, it would at least be a hue of red. This color is exclusively kept aside for the wedding day.

Significance: Red is auspicious. It is also the color that is related to passion and love. As per astrological significance, red is the color of Mars, the planet responsible for harmony in marriage.

2.Green
Green in any hue is eye-catching and soothing. The color is suitable for not just the newlywed but for the guests who attend the functions as well. In fact, for mehendi rituals, this is the favorite color for almost every Indian bride.

Significance: The green color symbolizes fertility, life, a harmonious existence, and freshness.

3.Yellow
Yellow is divine. We have often seen in the haldi ceremonies at weddings that yellow dominates the scene. It is not just the groom or the bride but everyone from the children to the eldest member of the family is clad in yellow.

Significance: Joy fused with a lot of energy and positive vibes.
4.Orange
If you mix red and yellow, we get the orange color. We have seen many such weddings where orange was predominant.

Significance: It adds a tangy vibrance to the ambiance and symbolizes happiness indicated by yellow and red’s energy in optimum doses.

5. Golden
Starting from the yesteryears, we have often seen at museums and in real life that golden used to be the color for weddings. The attire was complemented by heavily adorned gold jewelry.

Significance: Adds pomp and show galore to the occasion.

1. Opt for breathable fabrics:

title: Summer fashion hacks to stay cool
excerpt:
As summers are coming, it is the right time to shift to light, floaty summer dresses. Summer is the right time to fill your wardrobe with some light pastel-colored clothes. content:
As summers are coming, it is the right time to shift to light, floaty summer dresses. Summer is the right time to fill your wardrobe with some light pastel-colored clothes. With some little changes you can keep yourself stylish and trendy this summer. Here are a few of the summer fashion hacks to stay cool and trendy:

1. Beat the heat with loose clothing:

When it’s hot out, the last thing you want is clingy, sweaty clothes sticking to your body. Loose-fitting clothes in playful prints are a great addition to your summer wardrobe. For women, floaty dresses are a great statement-makers whether they are heading for work or attending luncheon. Women can go for loose T-shirts or kurti for casual outings. For men, relaxed fit t-shirts and shorts are the go-to weekend outfit for hot summer days. For an office look, they can team half-sleeved shirts with a pair of relaxed trousers.

2. Go for light colours :

Summers call for brighter hues. If your summer wardrobe needs to be revved up, opt for white, blue, pink, lighter hues of green, blue, and turquoise. Not only do lighter shades reflect back the heat and keep you cooler ,but they also help everyone put their best fashion forward during the sweltering weather. When heading for work, opt for solids or outfits with minimal prints. Women can wear light coloured kurti- palazzo or light colour cotton saree for work. For weekends you can choose a floral print T-shirt and keep it light.

3. Use adorable accessories for sun protection :

Accessories are one of the best ways to take your style quotient several notches higher. When done right, accessories such as a wide-brimmed hat, sunglasses, and scarves can amp up the overall look. Women can use bandanas to either tie their hair or cover their heads. This way, they protect their hair from direct UV rays. For men there are various kinds of hats and caps to cover their head and look stylish. They can wear sunglasses to complete their look.

4. Give the perfect finishing with a trendy bag:

A completely stylish look is incomplete without a bag. For a minimalist look, opt for a crossbody bag. If you are heading to the beach, carry a tote bag so that you can carry all your summer essentials such as sunscreen, sunglasses and water bottle.
To keep the summers stylish, men can go for side carrying laptop bags to complete their office look.

title: Checklist for Holi – Do’s & Don’ts Clothes for Holi
excerpt:
The festival of colours, Holi, is around the corner. It’s that time of the year when people look forward to eating gujiyas and playing with colours and water balloons. Holi is the perfect to festivaleither go for all-white clothes or vibrant coloured clothing. If you wish to know how you can ace your Holi look … 
Continue reading “Checklist for Holi – Do’s & Don’ts Clothes for Holi”
content:
The festival of colours, Holi, is around the corner. It’s that time of the year when people look forward to eating gujiyas and playing with colours and water balloons. Holi is the perfect to festivaleither go for all-white clothes or vibrant coloured clothing. If you wish to know how you can ace your Holi look without compromising on practicality, here’s a checklist of Holi —- Do’s & Don’ts clothes for holi

Do’s

Wear full sleeves kurta and denims:

Be it men or women, the best way to slay a cool Holilook is to wear a full-sleeve white kurta and a pair of denims. Not only does this ensemble look chic and elegant, but it also protects the skin from harsh colours. Bright orange and yellow are also some go-to colours for Holi outfits, if white is not your first preference. Complete this look with oversized sunglasses.

Wear head scarves:

Hair accessories such as caps, bandana and head scarves can add an interesting look to men or women’sholi dress. A colourful head accessory will give the Holi outfit the perfect look and protect the hair from colours.

Go for cotton T-shirts:

Cotton is one of the best fabrics to wear on Holi. It is light, breathable and comfortable. Cotton is a quick-drying fabric. Team up white, blue or yellow T-shirts with a pair of blue denims. If you are wearing white denims, opt for darker colored T-shirts to let the contrast pop.

Take proper hair and skin care:

Before heading out to play Holi, it is important to take care of the skin and hair. Apply a good amount of oil to the hair so that colours don’t stick too much. Use a hydrating moisturiser on the body.

Don’ts

Do not wear heavy footwear:

Avoid wearing heavy shoes or heels during holi celebrations. To enjoy this festival to the fullest it is best to wear flip flops which are comfortable yet light at the same time.

Avoid wearing makeup:

While everyone wants their Holi pictures to come out amazing, it is best to not use makeup. Cosmetics, when cosmeticscome into contact with colours, can cause allergic reactions.

Do not wear jewellery:

During Holi celebrations, it is best to avoid wearing jewellery. Metal necklaces or chains may react with water and colours causing skin irritation. Those who want to add a playful look to the Holi outfit should wear sleek and minimalisticjewellery.

Head to M Baazar to get the wardrobe essentials that you need for Holi.

content:
As winter sets in, it’s time to fill your wardrobe with clothing essentials that can be combined to create outfits that are classy, comfortable, and practical. Today, we are sharing with you winter essentials every wardrobe needs for creating stylish and cozy look all through the winter:

Winter season wardrobe essentials for Men

1. Long coats:

When it comes to creating a fashionable winter look, men can always count on long coats. Black, grey and beige long coats with their clean aesthetics are classy. For a semi-formal look, pair a long coat with jeans and boots. For a more formal look, team it trousers and black shoes.

2. Puffer jackets

No winter wardrobe is complete without a puffer jacket. A lightweight jacket can be easily paired with a T-shirt and a pair of denims. On chilly days, you can swap T-shirt with a cozy knitwear. Blue, black, yellow, beige and brown are some great colors to buy.

3. Muffler

A muffler goes well with jackets, coats and sweaters. A muted muffler is perfect to tone down a bright outfit while an eye-catching muffler can elevate the simple ensemble.

4. Thermal innerwear

One of the best ways to stay stylish in winters is to wear thermal innerwear. A thermal wear is necessary for feeling warm and cozy during the cold weather. The best part is it an be worn under any outfit as thermal innerwear are available in several colors such as black, beige, white and grey.

Winter season wardrobe essentials for Women

1. Woolen pullovers and sweaters

Pullovers and buttoned sweaters are chic and convenient. For a comfy and cozy look, pair it with a nice fitted T-shirt, denim and winter boots.

2. Long coat

Long coats are a must-have for an uber-stylish and sophisticated look. Team it with a pair of jeans and long boots. For an ethnic look, wear it with a well-pleated saree and high heels.

3. Shawl

Embroidered shawls add an ethereal and elegant look to any ensemble. They are perfect for casual as well as formal occasions. They can be easily paired with sarees and salwar kameez.

4. Thermal innerwear

The thermal innerwear with a form-fitting cut keeps the body warm, without lending a bulky look. Available in different colours, thermal inner wear can be easily worn under salwar kameez and Western outfits such as dresses and jeans and T shirt.

This winter, visit MBazaar to get all your winter wardrobe essentials!

Lookbook for Men

When it comes to ethnic wear, the first thing that comes to mind is a sherwani. A bright coloured sherwani is a great pick for Diwali. Sherwani will give you a regal look. And finally, complete your Diwali look with formal shoes or juttis.

Feel comfortable and look trendy in a short kurta and a pair of jeans. If you want to keep the ethnic look a bit mellow, this is exactly what you need. Instead of denims, you can also opt for chinos. For the little boys, a mini suit set is a great pick. Amp up their look with classic and chic accessories. Pair it with contrasting sneakers and a tie to immediately give the little champ a dapper look! Or a light coloured pocket square will add more charm to the outfit.

1. Traditional sarees: Saree is a quintessential Puja outfit. Sarees with heavy borders can accentuate a look. Use the right accessories such as gajras around the bun and jhumkas to complete the look.
2. Palazzos and kurtas: Nothing can beat the elegance of a salwar-kurta look. Complement this look with a pair of heels and a chic handbag.

3. Pair kurtis with shrugs: While wearing ankle-length kurtas, unite it up with a summer shrug. For footwear, go for sandals and create a head-turning look.

4. Go Denim: Denim jeans go beautifully with ethnic kurtas. Complete the look with colourful earrings and bright lip colours.

5. Light lehengas: Light lehengas are perfect for morning ‘aartis’. Lehengas with pastel and soft colours set the tone for a festive mood. Wear bangles and a colourful bindi to channelise the desi vibe.

For men:

1. Kurta pyjama: A classic set of kurta-pyjamas is an ideal ensemble to look traditional and stylish. It is comfortable and ideal to wear during Durga Puja lunch with family and friends.

2. Slim fit shirts: For a dapper look, wear solid coloured slim fit shirts with formal trousers. Make sure you complete the look with a pair of oxford shoes.

3. Printed casual shirts: If the Puja afternoon gets sunny, switch to a cool casual look. Wear light coloured printed shirts with a pair of cotton trousers.

4. Polo necked T-shirt: complete the look with a pair of leather sneakers and sunglasses.

For kids: (girls)

1. Salwar suits: Gorgeous and easy-to-carry, salwar suits are ideal outfits while taking part in Puja rituals.
2. Frocks: Layered frocks give a barbie-doll look. With cute hair accessories and a nice pair of shoes, frocks give a perfect look for the occasion.

3. Indo western lehengas: Trendy lehengas with Indo-western tops are stylish and yet comfortable.

4. Dungarees: If you are planning a long drive on a Pujo eve, doll up your girl with a comfortable pair of dungarees and matching shoes.

For kids: (Boys)

1. Kurta Pyjama (with a jacket): Little boys look adorable in kurta-pyjamas. To complete the look, pair up the attire with kolhapuri chappals.

2. Hooded half shirt: To pull off a casual and comfortable look, hooded half shirts are easy options. Perfect for a not-so-hot Pujo weather, a hooded shirt goes fantastic with a pair of denims and any casual shoes.

3. Casual tees with jeans: One can never go wrong with a casual T-shirt with a pair of jeans.

For Footwear and accessories:

1. Black heels: A pair of classic black heels never go out of style. Be it saree or a salwar suit, black heels add charm to outfits.

2. Casual shoes: Durga Puja is all about visiting friends, dancing with all the joy, and a lot of roaming around. To keep things comfortable, keep a pair of casual shoes ready.


3. Colour pop handbags: You can complement your ethnic outfit with a colourful handbag.

This Durga pujo, come visit M Baazar and give your wardrobe a makeover!

title: Unlock Happiness with M Baazar – Monsoon Fashion Guide
excerpt:
Nothing says joy like the monsoon. The raindrops on the windows and the smell of wet earth are just what you need to feel good after dealing with months of sweltering heat. However, puddles and muddy streets can make dressing up a bit challenging. But we won’t let the monsoon dampen your sense of style. … 
Continue reading “Unlock Happiness with M Baazar – Monsoon Fashion Guide”
content:
Nothing says joy like the monsoon. The raindrops on the windows and the smell of wet earth are just what you need to feel good after dealing with months of sweltering heat. However, puddles and muddy streets can make dressing up a bit challenging. But we won’t let the monsoon dampen your sense of style. With the right clothing, you can ace your fashion game.

Check out our easy styling tips to look chic no matter what the weather outside is:

Check out the ultimate monsoon guide:

Fashion tip 1: Choose the right fabric

During monsoon, it is quite impossible not to get drenched when you step out. This is why choosing light fabric makes sense. Cotton, linen, crepe and other light fabrics are excellent for the humid weather. They are not only breezy but they also dry easily.

For men:  Amp up your casual game with comfortable and light shirts and T-shirts. Pair Half-sleeves shirts and T-shirts with rugged jeans or joggers and you are good to go.

For women: Stay steer clear of tight clothings. Free-flowing floral dresses, cotton sarees, skirts, culottes and cropped pants will make everyone go ‘wow’. Give a twist to your attire by pairing culottes with short kurtis.

Fashion tip 2: Say hello to shorts and joggers

It’s time to bring them out your shorts and joggers. When paired with a nice top, you’re going to look dapper.

For men: Dark-hued shorts and jogger pants are ideal as they go well with the weather. Blue, black, steel grey and brown are perfect shades for lowers. Avoid wearing light colours for bottoms as they may get dirty easily.

For women:  Shorts, capris, skirts and culottes are comfortable and fashionable. Pair them with a beautiful T-shirt or blouse tops for a killer look. Dress in layers —- wear a summer jacket over the top.

Fashion tip 3: Make a statement with bright colours

Monsoon is the perfect season to bring out bright coloured outfits. Orange, yellow, turquoise blue and fuschia are some colours that will make you stand out from the crowd.

For men: For an uber-cool look, pair a button-downed shirt with chinos or casual pants. For a vacation vibe, let the tropical print do the magic. The best way to pull off the look is to balance prints and solids. For an everyday casual look, pair a printed shirt with a pair of trousers. For a semi-casual look, tone down the vibrancy of the tropical shirt by wearing it with a blazer.

For women: A lively outfit is all you need to lift your spirits on a gloomy rainy day. For work, pick kurta in bold hues and floral prints and match them with subtle colours. For special occasions or weekends, go glam with floral dresses and brightly coloured pants and top.

Fashion tip 4: Rock the ethnic look with the right garments

Lightweight kurtas are great choices to slay an ethnic look during the monsoon. Contrasting colours can nail a cheerful look.

For men: Short kurtas teamed with pants or pajamas or shirt-style kurtas over a pair of trousers can make a style statement this monsoon. Quick-dry cotton kurtas are the best choice.

For women: Pick mid-length light fabric kurta or a short kurti and team them with monsoon-friendly culottes, palazzo pants and ankle-length leggings.

To stay trendy this monsoon, visit M Bazaar.

title: The Latest Women’s Fashion Trends in 2021
excerpt:
The COVID-19 pandemic has dramatically changed lives and fashion is no exception. The year 2021 is seeing people define their personal style while keeping comfort in mind. If you want to keep with the latest looks, here are the fashion trends you must ace: Floral dresses: One of the easiest to pull off, floral dresses … 
Continue reading “The Latest Women’s Fashion Trends in 2021”
content:
The COVID-19 pandemic has dramatically changed lives and fashion is no exception. The year 2021 is seeing people define their personal style while keeping comfort in mind. If you want to keep with the latest looks, here are the fashion trends you must ace:

  • Floral dresses: One of the easiest to pull off, floral dresses are trending these days. Floral dresses, maxi style and knee-length are comfortable, effortless and yet stylish. A floral dress can be complemented with jewellery and heels for a formal event. If you are heading out with friends, throw it on with a pair of sneakers and you are good to go.
  • Casual clothing: Easy to style, casual clothing is becoming a staple for many. T-shirts are comfortable and look elegant while working from home or office. They go well with a pair of denims or joggers or cargo pants. T-shirts and joggers are a perfect combination of fashion and functionality. They are a wardrobe essential as they are comfortable and dapper-looking. For a super chic look, layer the T-shirt and the jogger with an unbuttoned shirt.
  • Colour blocking: Colour blocking is in vogue. Yellow, fuchsia, orange, magenta and turquoise — these are not just colours you wear. When you wear these bold colours, you make a bold fashion statement. A great way to pull off a colour blocking look is to pick bright, single-coloured pieces to create a statement look and then style it with accessories in different colours. Though there are no rules when it comes to colour blocking, fuchsia works well with magenta, red is the perfect partner for green and yellow complements blue.
  • Cotton kurtis: A great alternative to T-shirts, cotton kurtis are ideal for Indian summer. Slightly longer than regular T-shirts, kurtis and denims a feminine touch. Kurtis not only look stylish but they also keep you comfortable all day long. They can be easily paired with denims, palazzo pants or white capris.

title: Revamp your wardrobe with these Winter Wear Essentials
excerpt: The change that occurs from winter to summer is more clear than the one from summer to winter. From dry skin to the wardrobe, everything needs a major update and sensitive touch in the dry winter.
content:
The change that occurs from winter to summer is more clear than the one from summer to winter. From dry skin to the wardrobe, everything needs a major update and sensitive touch in the dry winter. From men to women, everyone needs an upgrade during winter. While woollen wears are still trending in fashion today, it’s important to make sure you have all the stylish warm apparel in your wardrobe to rock the chilling weather. There are many options to choose today, from coats to jackets and overalls. To help you make this transition smoothly, here’s a list of five essential winter wear that you can choose to appear more stylish and less dull this winter.

Woollen Coats: Woollen coats though are not new into fashion but can be used by both men and women as a style statement and to keep you warm. They have been mostly used as a cover-up on your apparel but comes in numerous styles today. They can be asymmetrical, long, short and even crop sized for women. The bold colours of modern woollen coats make them extremely trending among the young generation. You can wear it all buttoned up or choose to wear it as a jacket. Before choosing a woollen coat always make sure to buy a size larger as it looks more comfortable.

Polished Blazer: If you are looking for something that gives you a touch of fashion yet has a formal look then go for this polished blazer. They come in contrast and shaded colours and have a comfortable lining to keep your body warm. These tailored blazers are good for everyday wear and are extremely stylish for both men and women. The broad collars of these jackets give you a slimming effect and have a pepped up look.

Puffer Coats: Though not a new style, but puffer coats are always in fashion. These puffed apparels come with a feather-filled layer and are very cosy. They come in trending patterns and designs and can be found according to your fit and for your complete family. Some Puffer coats can be worn inside-out giving you an extra benefit. You can style your puffer coat with a cropped silhouette.

Leather Jacket: One of our favourite winters wears is a leather jacket. Every girl or boy needs to have at least one leather jacket in the closet to give them a classic look. It is the perfect look for your Christmas party or New Year’s Eve and comes in classic stylish colours to suit everyone’s taste. Leather jackets are very warm and have numerous chains to give a trending look.

Snugging Sweater Dress: If you love to wear a woollen that keeps snugging to your body then this dress is perfect for you. Made from extremely soft and warm wool, sweater dresses are trending today.

They come mostly in a single, solid colour and gives a traditional, knitted look that is envious. Pair your dress with a pair of formal shoes or a loafer and hit off instantly.

title: Latest Trends for the Festive Season !
excerpt: It’s almost time for the favorite time of the year for us Bengalis ! Let’s welcome Maa Durga with the following styling tips and add more joy this festive season.
content:
Whether you have realized it or not, but the essence of the festive season has already made the lukewarm air of September quite aromatic. Being a true Bengali, then you may have probably felt the sensation of the arrival of Maa Durga among us.

So how are you going to prepare for this year’s Durga Pooja? Will you choose the ‘same old, same old’ combination of formal wear? Or do you want to try something unique and extravagant? Make sure to go through the following suggestions to drive all of your dilemmas away.

The dressing trends for men have been quite the same for the last few years. So, let’s start our venture with them. The tradition of wearing a white shirt and a pair of black or navy blue trousers have been on the scene for quite some years.

As a type of official wear module, it does look pretty simple but eye-catching. However, if you want to go a little bit over the moon, then you can also opt for a combination of traditional and western wear. In this aspect, wearing a Punjabi with a dark-colored denim tends to be everyone’s choice.

However, if you want, then you can also change the style by wearing a plain Kurta on top and a checkered trouser as bottoms. Sounds perfect, doesn’t it? We bet it does!

Now, let’s move on to the trends for women. Unlike the men’s wear, the combinations for women usually tend to be a lot fresher and versatile. However, among them, the mishmash of embroidered Kurtis and Sharara seems to be pretty popular.

If you do pair your outlook up with proper jewelry pieces, then you will definitely look like a princess. However, if Sharara doesn’t fit your choice, then you can also try out Palazzos. Make sure to wear stilettos and a pendant necklace with it to brighten up your looks even more.

Nonetheless, if you do have a traditional sense of style, then you can also go for the forever- young combo of Kurta and trousers. You can amp up your styling a little bit more by pairing yourself up with flat sandals and tassel earrings.

As a Bengali, saree probably should be one of the must-haves on your ‘ladies wear’ list. But what are you going to choose among the sea of options? Well, in this case, we would ask you to go with the flow. Choose something that looks stunning on you.

If you wish to make things a bit little out of the box, then you can also ditch the blouse and wear a shirt or crop-top instead. It will exhibit the individuality of your fashion sense a lot more prominently.

Let’s wrap up our suggestions about Pujo wearables with the kids. So, for boys, you can go for a full-sleeve solid color t-shirt and pair it up with a jeans that has some decent patchworks and, you are all set.

On the other hand, for girls, going for anything with vibrant color should be the ideal choice. If you do want to maintain the modernity along with traditions, then Anarkali, too, should be an excellent option for you. You can also go for the mid-sized skirts in case the season of monsoon decides to come back!

Make sure to pair up with some jewelry models as well to add a touch of innocent glamour to your overall look!

title: Enjoy this auspicious occasion of Rakshabandhan with more style and happiness !
excerpt: Rakshabandhan is around the corner. The festival signifies the beautiful bond of a brother and a sister. With the festive vibes setting in, it is important that you keep your styling and clothing in sync. Thus, we’ll be talking mainly about how you can style yourself this Rakhi season to surmount the happiness with confidence.
content:
Rakshabandhan is around the corner. The festival signifies the beautiful bond of a brother and a sister. With the festive vibes setting in, it is important that you keep your styling and clothing in sync. Thus, we’ll be talking mainly about how you can style yourself this Rakhi season to surmount the happiness with confidence. Amongst all the festivity, what you wear is equally important. Your comfort is decided by what you wear, so choose wisely.

 

Here’s what you can wear this Rakhi

Here is a comprehensive list of the styling and clothing ideas that you can adapt this Rakshabandhan to make the occasion more memorable and cherish each moment with love and happiness.

Ethnic Wear will resonate with the Festive Vibes

The festive season has just arrived and so has the confusion of what to pick and what not to wear. Choose ethnic wear like kurta for men and sarees for women to suit the ethnic aura. You should choose colors that are vibrant and religious. Bright shades of red, orange, and yellow can be good options. However, if you are a minimalist, then go for cool variants like white, or lighter shades of yellow.

Loose clothing will comfort you more

Prefer wearing clothes that are a bit lose and make space for your body to breathe freely. The summer is at peak and wearing tight clothes might lead to irritation and fatigue. Thus, resort to clothes that are comfortable yet sophisticated. Wear simple and elegant clothes. Merchandise with heavy work of stones or embroidery may lead to increased sweating. Thus, simple and sober should be the choice.

Let your Kids free with the clothes they choose!

Although its festive season, kids can resort to their own choice. Don’t burden the kids to wear shimmering and sparkling clothes. Let them enjoy the festivity with comfort. Kid’s wear can include casual clothing or cotton kurta for comfort and ethnicity. They can wear
breathable and light clothes that will be complemented by the glistening rakhis they would tie on.

Wear semi-formal to suit the office environment

For people who are going to the office, style should not be a hindrance to the clothes you wear. Semi-formal is the perfect option as it will give a sense of festive belonging along with maintaining congruency with the formal atmosphere of the workplace. You can wear a kurta paired with jeans. Girls can either pick a traditional salwar kurta or semi-Indian fusion of the traditional wear.

Before Wrapping Up….

Own what you wear! In the end, your clothes do not define your character but they definitely decide your comfort and ease. So choose wisely the types of clothes you wear this festive season. Try to go for light shades and comfortable fit clothes. Do not choose too mainstream ethnic wear as it may be difficult to carry. Just own what you wear and enjoy every bit of it. Style up and be fashionable. After all the trends must be defined by you.

title: Update Your Wardrobe for the Monsoon !
excerpt: Monsoon is in full swing but do not let the rain dampen your fashion sense. Clothes reveal a person’s personality and it is thus important to be well-groomed and have a good sense of style. A person who is well dressed has a greater level of confidence.
content:
Monsoon is in full swing but do not let the rain dampen your fashion sense. Clothes reveal a person’s personality and it is thus important to be well-groomed and have a good sense of style. A person who is well dressed has a greater level of confidence. Being fashionable and trendy is the demand of today’s world. But in this season of monsoon it is quite laborious to wear all kinda of outfits like the other seasons. So we have to update our wardrobe accordingly so as to keep up with the fashion trends.

Here we have some helpful hacks by which you can update your wardrobe for the monsoon season!

Talking about kids wear: In this monsoon season kids should wear clothes that are light and are comfortable. They should avoid wearing heavy clothes such as denim. Wearing short-cotton clothes like skirts, shorts and T-shirts can prove to be really helpful.

Cotton clothes

Wearing cotton clothes in the monsoon season is one of the best ways to deal with it. Monsoon is a season of joy for kids and therefore they should wear clothes that do not spoil their joy.

Light dresses

Kids should be made to wear light dresses in monsoons such as T-shirts,shorts, skirts and frocks. Heavy dresses like synthetic and denim clothes can be much hectic to manage as they take time to dry up.

Bright colours and Wobbly clothes

Bright colors are cheerful and so is the season of monsoon. Wearing bright coloured outfits pleases both: the one who is wearing the outfit as well as the one who is looking at the person. Wearing loose and wobbly outfits can help a lot as they dry up quickly compared to tight outfits which protects the kid from falling ill.

 

Now talking about Men’s wear. Men should wear a kind of trendy as well as a classy outfit which may include a printed cotton shirt and a pair of cotton trousers. This would make up to be a perfect combo. Heavy clothes such as a blazer should be avoided. If necessary you can carry it in a case and wear it up.
In rainy season men should prefer to wear dark colors like brown, grey, and orange as they reflect your personality in a positive direction. The most important thing to keep in mind is to focus on cropped trousers and chinos. A casual look with a vibrant color T-shirt and Chinos or ripped jeans could be handy as well as stylish.

Women wear should include some light and trendy dresses during this season. Wearing rain boots in monsoon season is trendy as well as it will protects you against water logging. Women especially need to carry a lot of stuff in their purse and bags. And carrying a waterproof bag in this season of monsoon would be a perfect decision. Wearing beaded necklaces makes you look cool and fashionable. Flip-flops are useful for wearing in light rain as it it prevents your fancy wear from getting spoilt in rain.
